definitions.text: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.
examples: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.

definitions.text: To grow roots; to enter the earth, as roots; to take root and begin to grow.
examples: The cuttings are starting to root.

definitions.text: To prepare, oversee, or otherwise cause the rooting of cuttings.
examples: We rooted some cuttings last summer.

definitions.text: To turn up or dig with the snout.
examples: A pig roots the earth for truffles.

definitions.text: To seek favour or advancement by low arts or grovelling servility; to fawn.
An act of rummaging or searching.
To cheer (on); to show support (for) and hope for the success of. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: I'm rooting for you, don't let me down!
